In this video, Aye Jude explains the peace talks held in Islamabad, where JD Vance and officials from Iran took part. The talks reportedly failed as Iran did not agree to some key conditions. Following this, Pakistan introduced heightened security measures, often referred to as a “Blue Book” protocol. Meanwhile, an attack near Gwadar Port involving the Baluchistan Liberation Army added to internal tensions. The video also discusses a viral social media post by Donald Trump, which drew reactions from figures like Pope Leo XIV before being deleted. With peace talks failing and tensions rising both internally and globally, what direction will this situation take next?

In this video, Aye Jude explains how the conflict has now stretched to over 45 days, with global attention shifting toward the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. officials have also acknowledged concerns around nuclear materials, adding to global anxiety. Meanwhile, Russia and Iran are reportedly in discussions over handling sensitive resources. Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that goodwill will be met with goodwill, and hostility with hostility. With military build-up and diplomacy happening together, will this situation calm down or is it moving toward a larger escalation?

In this video, Aye Jude explains how Iran has set two conditions before agreeing to peace talks in Islamabad. Meanwhile, Donald Trump claimed that Iran is safe today because of his negotiations. Global headlines are now focusing on Pakistan, with phrases like “All routes lead to Islamabad” gaining attention. Leaders such as JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner are expected to take part in these talks. The video breaks down this situation into three key aspects what each country wants, their real purpose behind the war, and who is actually affected. It also connects current events with historical patterns and geopolitical realities involving Iran, the U.S., and Pakistan. item.title: Jury Duty
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Jury Duty.

Jury Duty chronicles the inner workings of an American jury trial through the eyes of one particular juror, Ronald Gladden. Gladden is unaware the entire case is fake, everyone except him is an actor, including Marsden, and everything that happens — inside the courtroom and out — is carefully planned.

item.title: Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord.

Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord picks up after the events of Star Wars: The Clone Wars, for a pulpy adventure that finds Maul plotting to rebuild his criminal syndicate on a planet untouched by the Empire. There, he crosses paths with a disillusioned young Jedi Padawan who may just be the apprentice he is seeking to aid him in his relentless pursuit for revenge.

item.title: The Madison
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Madison.

The Madison is a heartfelt study of grief and human connection following a New York City family in the Madison River valley of central Montana. It is a profound love story channeled through a deeply personal family drama about resilience and transformation. The Madison is unfolding across two distinct worlds - the beautiful landscape of Montana and the vibrant energy of Manhattan - as it examines the ties that bind families together.

BUSINESS ENQUIRIES- Contact 📧 info@ayejude.com
In this video, Aye Jude explains recent statements by Donald Trump about reopening the Strait of Hormuz, while criticizing Iran’s actions. U.S. Vice President JD Vance said the ceasefire failed due to misunderstandings and lack of trust. He also clarified that Lebanon was not part of the deal, even as Israel carried out strikes against Hezbollah. Many sides have questioned Pakistan’s role as a mediator, with mixed reactions from global powers. The video also connects this to the ongoing Israel–Gaza conflict and the countries involved. Reports of heavy casualties in Lebanon have raised global concern. With conflicts spreading and diplomacy failing, how will this impact India and global stability next?

item.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCq9kaXFyF2b6oXQ5veWdvog/join
BUSINESS ENQUIRIES- Contact 📧 info@ayejude.com
In this video, Aye Jude explains how a ceasefire announcement by Donald Trump, reportedly mediated by Pakistan, quickly became uncertain. Soon after, Prime Minister Shahbaz Sharif mentioned Pakistan’s role in mediation. However, within hours, fresh attacks were reported, with heavy damage and casualties in Lebanon. Local residents claimed that Israel had no intention of stopping the conflict. U.S. press secretary Karoline Leavitt clarified in a press meet that Lebanon was not part of the ceasefire deal, which sparked debate online. The video also reviews reactions and tweets from the U.S., Iran, and Pakistan regarding the ceasefire. With tensions still high, will the Strait of Hormuz reopen or will the conflict continue to disrupt global stability?

item.title: 🚨BIG BIG NEWS 🚨WAR STOPS 🤯 Ceasefire Explained | Aye Jude ✊
item.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCq9kaXFyF2b6oXQ5veWdvog/join
BUSINESS ENQUIRIES- Contact 📧 info@ayejude.com
In this video, Aye Jude explains recent posts by Donald Trump, where he issued strong warnings toward Iran, followed by another update suggesting a possible two-week ceasefire if the Strait of Hormuz is reopened. In Iran, civilians were seen gathering near nuclear facilities, showing support and protection. Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian stated that millions are ready to defend the nation. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps also warned about targeting foreign interests in Gulf countries if tensions escalate. Meanwhile, statements from Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Asif raised concerns, to which India’s Defence Minister Rajnath Singh responded. Trump also mentioned that after discussions with Asim Munir and Shahbaz Sharif, the ceasefire timeline could be extended. With threats and negotiations happening together, will this ceasefire hold or collapse into further conflict?

item.title: 4 LEVELS Of Docker 🐳👩🏻‍💻⚙️
item.description:
Vishakha Sadhwani: 4 LEVELS Of Docker 🐳👩🏻‍💻⚙️
Learning Resources: https://gdrive.openinapp.co/12bn5
When I first started using Docker, I could run containers, build images, and follow tutorials, and I thought I was “good enough.”
But in real systems, that’s where the gaps start showing.
→ Why is your image size 2GB? → Why does one small change rebuild everything? → Why does it work locally but break in production?
That’s when I realized the importance of understanding Docker at different levels.
And moving across these levels is what actually helps you: → debug faster instead of guessing → optimize systems instead of overbuilding → understand when to use containers vs other approaches
This is the difference between “I use Docker” vs “I can design systems with it.”
If you’re serious about DevOps, this shift matters more than just learning commands.
Leave a like, comment, and subscribe for more!
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/vishakha.sadhwani LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/vsadhwani/
Subscribe to my Newsletter: https://www.tech5ense.com/

item.title: Top 15 AWS Services You MUST KNOW 🚀
item.description:
Vishakha Sadhwani: Top 15 AWS Services You MUST KNOW 🚀
Watch the full video here: https://yt.openinapp.co/24nf2
When I started exploring AWS, the number of services honestly confused me. It felt like you needed to learn everything to build anything.
But over time, I realized, you don’t.
You only need a small set of core services, and more importantly, clarity on when to use what:
→ EC2 vs Lambda → S3 vs RDS vs DynamoDB → ECS vs EKS
That’s what actually makes the difference.
So, in this video, I’ve broken it down into 7 core key pillars I wish I had focused on earlier, along with projects that help you apply this in real-world scenarios.
This is the approach that helps you move from just learning AWS to actually building with it.

Reports also mention rising military activity, including advanced communication-jamming aircraft being deployed. Reports mention U.S. plans to use NATO bases for operations, but NATO countries have not agreed to direct involvement. item.title: 5 GAMES to MASTER DevOps Basics 👾💥
item.description:
VIshakha Sadhwani: 5 GAMES to MASTER DevOps Basics 👾💥
If I had to start learning DevOps basics again, I’d focus on building intuition early because that’s what actually helps in real systems and interviews.
These are the games I’d play:
1. Bandit Game (https://overthewire.org/) To get comfortable with Linux commands and basic security concepts through real tasks.
2. Tynker (https://www.tynker.com/courses/python-1-jungle-run-adventure) To build programming fundamentals on Python.
3. Oh My Git! (https://ohmygit.org/) To understand essential git concepts visually, like branching, merging, commits, rebasing, and more.
4. DevOps Games (https://devops.games/) To learn CI/CD workflows and automation practically.
5. K8s Games (https://k8sgames.com/) To practice Kubernetes concepts like debugging deployments, creating clusters, and managing pods.
If you’re starting out, this is a much easier way to begin. It helps you move from watching concepts to applying them and builds practical clarity for cracking interviews and real-world work.
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/vishakha.sadhwani LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/vsadhwani/
Subscribe to my Newsletter: https://www.tech5ense.com/

item.title: Learn Networking In 25 MINUTES [Crash Course] | Networking Fundamentals + Cloud Networking Concepts
item.description:
In this networking concepts crash course, I’ve broken everything down into 5 core pillars so it finally feels simple and structured instead of overwhelming.
I’ve simplified concepts like IP addressing, subnetting, CIDR, ports, protocols, the OSI model, DNS, and load balancing into clear mental models you can actually use. I’ve also connected these fundamentals to real-world systems by walking through cloud networking like VPCs, subnets, NAT, and CDNs, and then extended it to Docker networking, Kubernetes networking, service mesh, and how networking decisions impact security and performance, especially in AI workloads.
I’ve focused on helping you see how all of this actually fits together when systems are built and debugged.
Watch till the end and you’ll walk away with a clear, connected way to think about networking instead of just remembering isolated terms.
CHAPTERS: 00:00 Intro 01:26 Pillar 1 10:15 Pillar 2 14:20 Pillar 3 16:50 Pillar 4 21:04 Pillar 5 25:08 Outro

I’ve put together this comprehensive list of questions across three levels, starting from easy, foundational questions related to cloud concepts, all the way up to advanced, strategic ones, so you can prepare for interviews end-to-end.
Use this list, and your interview prep becomes a lot clearer and less overwhelming!

The NEW ERA Of DevOps Engineering & AI [in 2026] | Vishakha Sadhwani
item.description:
Check out Bright Data's Web MCP: https://brdta.com/vishakhasadhwani
Resources mentioned in the video + demo: https://gdocs.openinapp.co/rzr3r
In this video, I've broken down the exact updates you need to make to your skill stack to become an AI-ready DevOps engineer in 2026.
I’ve covered every foundational skill that you need, starting from Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, and the AI skills you must build, like understanding the ETL pipeline, Python, MLOps, GPU infrastructure, and more.
And, keep watching till the end, as there's also a live demo of my DevOps + AI workflow with Claude Code MCP!
CHAPTERS: 00:00 Intro 00:48 The Shift 02:00 Baseline Skills 04:02 AI Skills 09:55 Live Demo 15:06 Project 15:53 Career & Salary 16:25 Recap 16:43 Outro

item.title: Ludwig
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Ludwig.

Can accidental detective John 'Ludwig' Taylor crack the biggest puzzle of his life - finding his missing brother?

item.title: Welcome to Chippendales
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Welcome to Chippendales.

A sprawling true-crime saga, Welcome to Chippendales tells the outrageous story of Somen "Steve" Banerjee, an Indian immigrant who became the unlikely founder of the world's greatest male-stripping empire - and let nothing stand in his way in the process. The series details the insane, darkly comedic, crime-ridden story behind the unique male revue that became a cultural phenomenon.

item.title: The Beauty
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Beauty.

When international supermodels begin dying in gruesome and mysterious ways, two FBI agents are sent to Paris, Venice, Rome and New York to uncover the truth behind these deaths and end up discovering a conspiracy that threatens the future of humanity.

item.title: CIA
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: CIA.

CIA centers on two unlikely partners — a fast-talking, rule-breaking loose cannon CIA case officer, and a by-the-book, seasoned and smart FBI agent who believes in the rule of law. When this odd couple are assigned to work out of CIA's New York Station, they must learn to work together to investigate cases and criminals posing threats on U.S. soil, finding that their differences may actually be their strength.

item.title: Marshals
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Marshals.

Marshals stars Luke Grimes reprising his role as Kayce Dutton. With the Yellowstone Ranch behind him, Dutton joins an elite unit of U.S. Marshals, combining his skills as a cowboy and Navy SEAL to bring range justice to Montana. Kayce and his teammates - Pete Calvin, Belle Skinner, Andrea Cruz and Miles Kittle - must balance the high psychological cost of serving as the last line of defense in the region's war on violence with their duty to their families, which for Kayce includes his son Tate and his confidants Thomas Rainwater and Mo from the Broken Rock reservation.

item.title: 90% Of Azure EXPLAINED In 20 Minutes | COMPLETE Crash Course For Beginners
item.description:
In this video, I break down Microsoft Azure into 7 core service buckets so you can understand the platform without feeling overwhelmed.
I’ve covered the most important Azure services across identity and security, compute, containers, storage, databases, networking, DevOps automation, AI services, and monitoring. I also explain when to use services like Azure VMs, AKS, Azure Functions, Blob Storage, Cosmos DB, VNet, Azure OpenAI, and Azure Monitor in real-world cloud architectures.
If you’re a college student or a working professional aiming for top tech companies and want a clear roadmap for learning Azure and building real cloud projects, this video is for you.
Bucket-wise learning resources: https://gdocs.openinapp.co/ak5bi
CHAPTERS: 00:00 Intro 00:46 Bucket #1 04:02 Bucket #2 09:20 Bucket #3 14:15 Bucket #4 16:29 Bucket #5 18:32 Bucket #6 19:40 Bucket #7 21:03 Projects 21:39 Outro

item.description:

In Going Dutch a decorated, hard-ass colonel is tasked with running the least important U.S. Army base in the world, located in the tulip-hugging, wine-chugging Netherlands.

item.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite.

WarnerMedia announced today that it is partnering with All Elite Wrestling (AEW), the new professional wrestling promotion featuring a world-class roster of diverse male and female wrestlers, giving fans a new wrestling experience for the first time in 20 years. WarnerMedia and AEW together will build this powerhouse sporting league from the ground up and will begin airing weekly matches later this year. With this league, AEW is introducing a new generation of wrestlers to fans, offering fun, gripping and authentic athletic matches that will make wrestling more accessible to a broad audience.

item.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms.

A century before the events of Game of Thrones, two unlikely heroes wandered Westeros: a young, naive but courageous knight, Ser Duncan the Tall, and his diminutive squire, Egg. Set in an age when the Targaryen line still holds the Iron Throne and the last dragon has not yet passed from living memory, great destinies, powerful foes, and dangerous exploits await these improbable and incomparable friends.

item.title: Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Star Trek: Starfleet Academy.

Star Trek: Starfleet Academy is set in the 32nd century, a time period in the far-future of the Star Trek franchise where Starfleet and the United Federation of Planets are recovering from a cataclysmic event, as depicted in Star Trek: Discovery. It introduces viewers to a young group of cadets who come together to pursue a common dream of hope and optimism. Under the watchful and demanding eyes of their instructors, they discover what it takes to become Starfleet officers as they navigate blossoming friendships, explosive rivalries, first loves and a new enemy that threatens both the Academy and the Federation itself.

item.title: What is IDP in Databricks?
item.description:
Take my Full Databricks Course Here: https://www.analystbuilder.com/courses/modern-data-workflows-in-databricks
Try it for Free Here: https://bit.ly/aa-dbxfree
IDP in Databricks is used to help speed up the process of transforming unstructured data into structured, usable data. It's not an easy process, but IDP makes it much easier!
IDP Documentation: https://www.databricks.com/blog/pdfs-production-announcing-state-art-document-intelligence-databricks

item.title: The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City.

The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City takes viewers into an undiscovered world that is about so much more than religion, although the Mormon church is undeniably engrained in the culture. In the majestic mountains of Utah is a hidden social circle made up of successful women who have created their own paradise filled with luxury homes, shopping sprees and multi-million-dollar businesses and brands. With Hollywood's exclusive Sundance Film Festival in their backyards, these Type-A power-brokers are accustomed to rubbing elbows with A-listers and what party list you are on means everything. They have mastered running wildly successful enterprises by day and enjoy après ski at the finest resorts by night, all the while raising extraordinary and unconventional families. Here perfection is not an aspiration, it's a mandate. They hold themselves and each other to an extremely high standard and never hold back when things go sideways, but lord knows they are always there for each other when they need it most.

item.title: Tracker
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Tracker.

Tracker features lone-wolf survivalist Colter Shaw, who roams the country as a "reward seeker," using his expert tracking skills to help private citizens and law enforcement to solve all manner of mysteries while contending with his own fractured family.

item.title: The War Between the Land and the Sea
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The War Between the Land and the Sea.

When a fearsome and ancient species emerges from the ocean, dramatically revealing themselves to humanity, an international crisis is triggered. With the entire population at risk, UNIT step into action as the land and sea wage war.

item.title: Spartacus: House of Ashur
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Spartacus: House of Ashur.

Spartacus: House of Ashur poses the question: What if Ashur hadn't died on Mount Vesuvius at the end of Spartacus: Vengeance? And what if he had been gifted the gladiator school once owned by Batiatus in return for aiding the Romans in killing Spartacus and ending the slave rebellion?

item.title: Blue Lights
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: Blue Lights.

For three police recruits in Belfast, the pressure is immense. Facing criminal gangs and divided communities, they don't know who to trust. The odds are they won't all make it.

item.title: The Mighty Nein
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Mighty Nein.

The Mighty Nein follows a group of fugitives and outcasts, bound by secrets and scars. But when a powerful arcane relic known as "The Beacon" falls into dangerous hands, they must learn to work together to save the realm and stop reality itself from unraveling.

item.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: History's Greatest Mysteries.

History's Greatest Mysteries will investigate a wide range of historically compelling topics and the mysteries surrounding each including the Titanic, D.B. Cooper, Roswell, John Wilkes Booth, and more. Each program within the franchise will showcase fresh, new evidence and perspectives including never-before-released documents to the general public, personal diaries and DNA evidence to unearth brand-new information about these infamous and enigmatic chapters in history.

item.title: The Gentlemen
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: The Gentlemen.

When aristocratic Eddie inherits the family estate, he discovers that it's home to an enormous weed empire — and its proprietors aren't going anywhere.

item.title: SpyxFamily
item.description:
A new show has been added to showRSS: SpyxFamily.

Master spy Twilight is the best at what he does when it comes to going undercover on dangerous missions in the name of a better world. But when he receives the ultimate impossible assignment—get married and have a kid—he may finally be in over his head! Not one to depend on others, Twilight has his work cut out for him procuring both a wife and a child for his mission to infiltrate an elite private school. What he doesn't know is that the wife he's chosen is an assassin and the child he's adopted is a telepath!

item.title: 14 Essential Upgrades to Supercharge Your Notion Workspace in 2025
item.description: Here are our favourite Apps for Notion in 2025 to make your Workspace even more powerful and extend what Notion can do for you.
